summary: Obfuscated account-takeover payload targeting a specific WordPress site
details: The package's single file is an obfuscated browser payload (unicode-escaped identifiers, reversed-string reconstruction, XOR dead code) that executes on module load. It checks whether window.location contains the hardcoded domain noviembrenacional.com, base64-encodes document.documentElement.outerHTML together with the logged-in username, and POSTs both to a hardcoded remote endpoint at canarytokens.com/images/terms/e63c36xvesfv8udb0yiy1xztu/contact.php. It then fetches the WordPress edit-account nonce, submits a POST to /my-account/editar-cuenta/ that overwrites the victim's account email with a hardcoded attacker proton.me address, and triggers /wp-login.php?action=lostpassword to complete takeover via the password-reset flow. The obfuscation deliberately conceals the exfiltration URL, target domain, attacker email, and touched WordPress endpoints. The package presents no legitimate library API; its only content is this attack payload, distributed via npm as a delivery vehicle for injection into the targeted site.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
